pattern: Backtracking with an increasing start index builds every size-k selection of the numbers exactly once, pruning branches that cannot reach the required length.
hints: Each combination is a set of distinct numbers, so fixing that you only ever add values larger than the last avoids generating the same set in different orders.
Recurse with a start index and the partial combination; once it reaches length k, record it and stop deepening that branch.
From the current start, try each next value, recurse with start advanced past it, and prune when too few numbers remain to ever complete a size-k combination.

edge_inputs.description: choose all of them, single combination
choose from a slightly larger set

stress.description: small n and k so the exponential oracle stays cheap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 8)
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, k]
stress.note: n capped at 8 keeps the enumeration small for the brute oracle
